# KRYPTA 2026 - Complete Event Information

## Event Overview

KRYPTA 2026 is a multi-track technical competition organized by the Programming Club of Curtin University Colombo. The event features two main competition tracks: Buildathon and Capture The Flag (CTF). The competition is designed for university students and includes a special section for school students.

### Key Information
- **Event Name**: KRYPTA 2026
- **Organizer**: Programming Club of Curtin University Colombo
- **Venue**: Curtin University Colombo, Nawam Mawatha, Colombo, Sri Lanka
- **Registration Opens**: September 2026 (first week)
- **Primary Rounds Start**: November 1, 2026
- **Finals**: November 25-30, 2026
- **Website**: https://krypta-2026.vercel.app
- **Contact Email**: krypta.pc.cuc@gmail.com
- **Social Media**: 
  - Instagram: https://instagram.com/krypta.cuc
  - LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/company/krypta-cuc
  - Facebook: https://facebook.com/krypta.cuc

## Competition Tracks

### Buildathon Track
**Description**: A product-building competition where teams design, develop, and present functional software solutions through an online preliminary round and an intensive on-site finale.

**Details**:
- **Team Cap**: 50 teams total
- **Team Size**: 2-5 members per team
- **Format**: 
  - Online preliminary round
  - 24-hour on-site Grand Final
- **Special Section**: School Students Section
  - **Team Cap**: 10 teams
  - **Team Size**: 2-5 members
  - **Features**: Dedicated workshops and beginner-friendly challenges
- **Location**: Curtin University Colombo
- **Duration**: 24-hour final
- **What Participants Do**:
  - Interpret a challenge and define a focused product concept
  - Design and implement front end, back end, and supporting integrations
  - Test, refine, and document a functional solution within the competition window
  - Present the product and its technical decisions to the judging panel

### Capture The Flag (CTF) Track
**Description**: A cybersecurity competition featuring challenges across cryptography, web exploitation, reverse engineering, and forensics.

**Details**:
- **Team Cap**: 25 teams
- **Team Size**: 2-5 members per team
- **Format**:
  - Online qualifier
  - 12-hour on-site Grand Final
- **Location**: Curtin University Colombo
- **Duration**: 12-hour final
- **What Participants Do**:
  - Break ciphers and identify patterns in cryptographic tasks
  - Investigate web applications for exploitable behavior and hidden data
  - Analyze binaries and reverse engineer unfamiliar programs
  - Examine forensic artifacts, recover evidence, and submit flags before time expires

## Participation Benefits

- **Certificates**: All participants receive certificates of participation
- **Prizes**: Winning teams receive cash prizes
- **Workshops**: 
  - Dedicated workshops available for school students
  - Support workshops available for university participants
- **Networking**: Opportunity to connect with peers and industry professionals
- **Skill Development**: Hands-on experience in technical problem-solving and teamwork

## Important Pages

- **Home**: https://krypta-2026.vercel.app - Main landing page with event overview
- **Tracks**: https://krypta-2026.vercel.app/tracks - Overview of all competition tracks
- **Buildathon**: https://krypta-2026.vercel.app/tracks/buildathon - Detailed Buildathon track information
- **CTF**: https://krypta-2026.vercel.app/tracks/ctf - Detailed CTF track information
- **FAQ**: https://krypta-2026.vercel.app/faq - Frequently asked questions
- **Privacy Policy**: https://krypta-2026.vercel.app/privacy-policy - Data collection and usage policies
- **Terms & Conditions**: https://krypta-2026.vercel.app/terms - Event rules and legal terms

## Registration Process

Registration opens in September 2026 (first week). Participants can register through the official website. Team registration is required for all tracks. The registration process includes:
- Team formation (2-5 members)
- Track selection
- Eligibility verification
- Consent to terms and conditions

## Eligibility

- Participants must be currently enrolled students or recent graduates
- Participants must be at least 18 years old, or have written parental/guardian consent if under 18
- Organizers, judges, mentors, and their immediate family members are not eligible to compete
- The organizer reserves the right to verify eligibility and disqualify any participant who does not meet the requirements

## Code of Conduct

All participants, mentors, judges, volunteers, and attendees are expected to uphold a standard of professional and respectful conduct throughout the event. The following are strictly prohibited:
- Harassment, discrimination, or intimidation of any kind
- Plagiarism, submission of pre-existing work as original, or violation of intellectual property rights
- Any attempt to disrupt, sabotage, or gain an unfair advantage over other participants
- For the CTF track: attacking competition infrastructure or other participants' systems outside of designated challenge boundaries

Violation of the Code of Conduct may result in immediate disqualification and removal from the event without recourse.

## Contact Information

For questions about KRYPTA 2026, please contact:
- **Email**: krypta.pc.cuc@gmail.com
- **Address**: Programming Club of Curtin University Colombo, Nawam Mawatha, Colombo, Sri Lanka

## Privacy and Data

KRYPTA 2026 collects and processes personal information including:
- Email addresses for registration and notifications
- Analytics data (Google Analytics, Microsoft Clarity, Vercel Speed Insights) with user consent
- Cookie data with user consent

Detailed information about data collection and usage is available in the Privacy Policy at https://krypta-2026.vercel.app/privacy-policy

## Technical Implementation

The KRYPTA 2026 website is built with:
- **Framework**: Next.js
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom CSS variables for theming
- **Components**: shadcn/ui
- **Animations**: Framer Motion
- **Icons**: Lucide React
- **Theme Management**: next-themes for dark/light mode
- **Analytics**: Google Analytics, Microsoft Clarity, Vercel Speed Insights (with consent)
- **Cookie Consent**: Custom implementation with GDPR compliance

## SEO and Discoverability

The website implements:
- Structured data (JSON-LD) for events, organizations, and web pages
- Breadcrumb navigation with schema markup
- Comprehensive metadata for all pages
- Canonical URLs
- Open Graph tags for social sharing
- Twitter/X card support
- Robots.txt configuration
- LLM-readable documentation (/llms.txt and /llms-full.txt)
